element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns roster mechanics and team composition
Dribbling and pass execution under pressure

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Real-time match simulation with natural athletic ebbs and flows; tension builds with proximity to goal, releases with clear possessions or goals

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory matches against lower-tier opposition; player learns fundamental controls and spatial management
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increasing opponent AI sophistication; matches against competitive teams require tactical adaptation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Championship-level teams with advanced defensive and offensive tactics; high-pressure scoring situations
notable_spikes: First championship match (increased opponent speed and coordination)

notable_level_patterns.description: Sequential match progression with team selection, match play across two halves, scoring objectives
Bracket-based advancement with escalating difficulty; regional/championship progression
Soccer pitch responds to player and AI positioning; ball physics and player momentum affect play outcomes

extraction_notes: Source provided regional differences and release information only. No detailed gameplay mechanics, level design sequences, or rhythm data documented in available source. Element introduction order inferred from typical soccer game flow. Spatial grammar and rhythm patterns extrapolated from sports-racing paradigm conventions.
